marketFocus: The ceasefire is holding on paper but cracking in practice. IRGC claimed Hormuz shipping stopped again after Israel violated the ceasefire in Lebanon. Iran's Parliament Speaker says 3 clauses violated. Iran demands crypto tolls for Hormuz passage. US troops staying in place. Formal talks in Islamabad on Saturday (Vance + Witkoff). Brent nursing losses but still above $91/bbl. Equities marginally lower as ceasefire euphoria wanes.

FOMC Minutes (March meeting) confirmed a hawkish hold: 'Many members said persistently higher oil prices could keep inflation elevated long enough to justify rate rises.' Fed Waller: 'if oil stays high, we may need to hike.' Fed Daly: 'inflation risks are tilted to the upside.' DXY recovering after yesterday's -1% drop. USD is regaining some lost ground as the ceasefire fragility narrative takes hold.

Key events today: Core PCE + Final GDP Q4 + Unemployment Claims at 13:30 FFT (CRITICAL). SNB Chairman Schlegel spoke at 09:00 FFT — no hawkish surprise. German Industrial Production missed (-0.3% MoM vs +0.9% exp). German Exports beat (+3.6% MoM). Spanish Industrial Production missed (-1.1% YoY vs +1.5% exp). JGB 5yr auction solid (b/c 3.58x). Japan releasing 20 days of strategic oil reserves.

USD.tone: FOMC Minutes Hawkish — DXY Recovering; Core PCE + Final GDP at 13:30 FFT Critical
USD.summary: USD is Bullish today. FOMC Minutes confirmed the hawkish hold: 'many members said persistently higher oil prices could keep inflation elevated long enough to justify rate rises.' Fed Waller: 'if oil stays high, we may need to hike.' DXY is recovering after yesterday's -1% drop as the ceasefire fragility narrative restores some haven demand. Core PCE + Final GDP at 13:30 FFT is the key catalyst — a hot Core PCE print would significantly reinforce the USD bullish case. Stagflation framework intact. Weekly Strong Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION ✅.
drivers: FOMC Minutes hawkish: 'persistently higher oil could justify rate rises' — Fed Waller confirms
DXY recovering after -1% drop — ceasefire fragility restores partial haven demand
Core PCE at 13:30 FFT — hot print would reinforce stagflation narrative and USD bulls
Final GDP Q4 + Unemployment Claims at 13:30 FFT — watch for any downside surprise
Weekly Strong Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅

EUR.tone: German Industrial Production Missed — EUR/USD Rangebound; No Directional Conviction
EUR.summary: EUR is Neutral today. German Industrial Production missed (-0.3% MoM vs +0.9% exp) — the industrial recovery remains fragile. German Exports beat (+3.6% MoM) and Trade Balance came in above expectations (19.8B vs 18.5B exp) — a partial offset. Spanish Industrial Production also missed (-1.1% YoY vs +1.5% exp). EUR/USD is rangebound near 1.1700. ECB hawkish repricing was pared back sharply on the ceasefire (only 2bps for April) but 45bps by year-end still priced. No directional conviction. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = NO alignment.
drivers: German Industrial Production MISSED: -0.3% MoM vs +0.9% exp — industrial recovery fragile
German Exports BEAT: +3.6% MoM — trade balance positive (19.8B vs 18.5B exp)
Spanish Industrial Production MISSED: -1.1% YoY vs +1.5% exp — broad EU industrial weakness
ECB hawkish repricing pared back — only 2bps for April; 45bps by year-end still priced
EUR/USD rangebound near 1.1700 — no directional conviction

GBP.tone: GBP/USD Stalled at 1.3440 — UK RICS House Price Balance Worst in 2+ Years; BOE Repricing Slightly Dovish
GBP.summary: GBP is Neutral today. GBP/USD stalled just above 1.3440 after yesterday's +1% rally. UK RICS House Price Balance came in at -23% (vs -18% exp) — the worst reading in over 2 years, reflecting the housing market stress from elevated rates. Halifax HPI also missed yesterday (-0.5% MoM). BOE is now pricing slightly dovish (27bps vs 30bps pre-market). The energy relief narrative from yesterday's ceasefire is fading as Hormuz remains contested. No fresh UK catalyst today. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = NO alignment.
drivers: GBP/USD stalled at 1.3440 — rally from yesterday fading as ceasefire fragility emerges
UK RICS House Price Balance: -23% vs -18% exp — worst in 2+ years; housing market stress
BOE pricing slightly dovish: 27bps vs 30bps pre-market — hawkish repricing pared back
Halifax HPI missed yesterday: -0.5% MoM vs +0.1% exp — housing headwind persists
No fresh UK catalyst today — quiet UK calendar

JPY.tone: USD/JPY Approaching 159.00 — BOJ Hawkish Backdrop, Japan Releasing Strategic Oil Reserves
JPY.summary: JPY is Bullish today. USD/JPY is approaching 159.00 as JPY continues to benefit from narrowing US-Japan yield differentials and the structural positive of lower oil prices for Japan's current account. BOJ Governor Ueda's comments were slightly dovish ('short and medium-term rates are clearly negative, accommodative conditions maintained') but the market is ignoring this — the hawkish repricing of the BOJ cycle remains intact. Japan is releasing 20 days of strategic oil reserves — a positive signal for energy security. JGB 5yr auction was solid (b/c 3.58x). Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION ✅.
drivers: USD/JPY approaching 159.00 — JPY benefiting from narrowing US-Japan yield differentials
Japan releasing 20 days of strategic oil reserves — energy security positive for JPY
JGB 5yr auction solid (b/c 3.58x) — domestic bond demand intact
BOJ hawkish backdrop intact — Shunto wages 5%+, tightening cycle underway
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅

AUD.tone: AUD/USD Holding Gains — Copper 3-Week High, China Import Quotas Expanded; Risk-On Partially Intact
AUD.summary: AUD is Bullish today. AUD/USD is holding yesterday's gains as the commodity complex remains supported. Copper is at a 3-week high and aluminium advanced. China was given additional crude import quotas to maintain fuel production — a positive for the China growth narrative which supports AUD via the commodity channel. Gold remains above $4,850/oz. The ceasefire fragility is a mild headwind but AUD's bullish momentum from yesterday is intact. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bullish = NO alignment (mismatch) — intraday only.
drivers: AUD/USD holding yesterday's gains — commodity complex remains supported
Copper at 3-week high, aluminium advanced — base metals recovery intact
China given additional crude import quotas — positive for China growth narrative
Gold above $4,850/oz — precious metals surge supports AUD via commodity channel
Weekly Bearish + Daily Bullish = NO alignment (mismatch) — intraday only, no swing setup

CAD.tone: Oil Above $91/bbl but Below Pre-Ceasefire Levels — CAD Rangebound; No Fresh Catalyst
CAD.summary: CAD is Neutral today. Brent is nursing losses above $91/bbl — still elevated versus pre-war levels but significantly below the $116/bbl peak. CAD loses its primary driver as the oil premium fades, but the absolute level still provides some support. Canada Services PMI remains in contraction (47.2). No fresh Canadian data today. Ivey PMI yesterday was the key catalyst. The net result is Neutral — oil support and domestic weakness cancel each other out. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = NO alignment.
drivers: Brent above $91/bbl but below $116/bbl peak — oil premium fading but still elevated
Canada Services PMI in contraction (47.2) — domestic weakness persists
No fresh Canadian data today — quiet CAD calendar
CAD rangebound — oil support and domestic weakness offsetting each other
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = NO alignment

CHF.tone: SNB Schlegel No Hawkish Surprise — Haven Flows Reversing; USD/CHF Recovering
CHF.summary: CHF is Bearish today. SNB Chairman Schlegel spoke at 09:00 FFT — no hawkish surprise delivered. CHF Unemployment Rate beat (3.1% vs 3.2% exp) — a minor positive but insufficient to shift the directional bias. Haven flows are reversing as the ceasefire partially holds. USD/CHF is recovering as the USD regains lost ground. SNB intervention cap limits CHF appreciation. The ceasefire fragility is a mild tailwind for CHF haven demand, but the dominant force is the USD recovery. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION ✅.
drivers: SNB Schlegel spoke at 09:00 FFT — no hawkish surprise; CHF loses potential catalyst
CHF Unemployment Rate BEAT: 3.1% vs 3.2% exp — minor positive overwhelmed by haven reversal
Haven flows reversing — ceasefire partially holds, reducing CHF safe-haven premium
USD/CHF recovering — USD regaining lost ground from yesterday's -0.7% drop
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅

NZD.tone: RBNZ Follow-Through — Governor Breman Confirms Rate Hike Cycle Accelerating; NZD Outperformer
NZD.summary: NZD is Bullish today. RBNZ Governor Breman confirmed the hawkish stance in follow-up comments: 'inflation expected to increase considerably in the near-term, will act decisively.' ASB Bank sees RBNZ hiking September AND December. NZD/USD is holding the gains from yesterday's hawkish RBNZ surprise. The ceasefire fragility is a mild headwind for NZD as a high-beta currency, but the RBNZ hawkish repricing is the dominant driver. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bullish = NO alignment (mismatch) — intraday only.
